subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — Middle of Year (MOY) Test 2
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For type-in questions, write only the number. For fill-in-the-blank equations, write the number that makes the equation true.

questions.questionText: What is 4 × 7?
questions.explanation: 4 groups of 7 is 28.

questions.questionText: The dots below are arranged in equal rows. How many dots are there in all?
questions.explanation: 6 rows of 3 is 6 × 3 = 18 dots.

questions.questionText: What is 45 ÷ 9?
questions.explanation: 45 shared into 9 equal groups is 5 in each group.

questions.questionText: Which multiplication fact matches the array shown below?
questions.explanation: There are 7 rows with 5 dots each, so 7 × 5 = 35.

questions.questionText: Lucas has 63 marbles. He puts them into 7 equal bags. How many marbles are in each bag? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: 63 ÷ 7 = 9 marbles in each bag.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 56 ÷ 7 = 8, so the blank is 7.

questions.questionText: Select all the expressions that have the same value as 6 × 7.
questions.explanation: 6 × 7 = 42. 7 × 6 = 42 (order does not change the product), and 6 added 7 times = 42. But 6 × 6 = 36 and 6 + 7 = 13, so those are not correct.

questions.questionText: A garden has 8 rows of carrots with 5 carrots in each row. Then 7 carrots are picked. How many carrots are left in the garden? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: First 8 × 5 = 40 carrots. Then 40 − 7 = 33 carrots left.

questions.questionText: Nina buys 6 boxes of crayons with 8 crayons in each box. She gives 9 crayons to her brother. How many crayons does she have left? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: First 6 × 8 = 48 crayons. Then 48 − 9 = 39 crayons left.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 73 − 46 = 27, so the blank is 27.

questions.questionText: What fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 4 equal parts. The point is on the 2nd mark, so it is 2/4.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: The bar has 4 equal parts and 3 are shaded, so 3/4 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: The circle has 6 equal parts and 4 are shaded, so 4/6 is shaded.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to 1/4?
questions.explanation: Bar A shows 1/4 shaded and Bar B shows 2/8 shaded with the same amount, so 2/8 = 1/4.

questions.questionText: Two same-size pizzas are cut into equal slices. One is cut into 6 slices and one is cut into 8 slices. Which is true about ONE slice from each pizza?
questions.explanation: When a pizza is cut into fewer slices, each slice is bigger. 6 slices are bigger than 8 slices, so 1/6 is bigger than 1/8.

questions.questionText: There are 20 grapes. One fourth (1/4) of them are green. How many grapes are green? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: To find 1/4 of 20, divide 20 into 4 equal groups: 20 ÷ 4 = 5 green grapes.

questions.questionText: Select all the fractions that are equal to one whole.
questions.explanation: When the top number equals the bottom number, the fraction equals one whole: 3/3, 5/5, and 6/6 all equal 1. But 2/4 equals one half, so it is not correct.

questions.questionText: Each square is 1 square unit. What is the area of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 4 × 3 = 12 square units.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 8 + 5 + 8 + 5 = 26 cm.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is 10 cm long and 3 cm wide. Which statement is true about its area and perimeter?
options: The area is 13 sq cm and the perimeter is 30 cm
The area is 30 sq cm and the perimeter is 26 cm
The area and perimeter are both 30
The area is 26 sq cm and the perimeter is 30 cm
questions.explanation: Area = 10 × 3 = 30 square cm. Perimeter = 10 + 3 + 10 + 3 = 26 cm.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 90 − 58 = 32, so the blank is 32.

questions.questionText: A library program starts at 3:40 and lasts 35 minutes. What time does it end?
questions.explanation: From 3:40, add 20 minutes to reach 4:00, then 15 more minutes to reach 4:15. So it ends at 4:15.

questions.questionText: Lunch begins at 11:35 and ends at 12:10. How many minutes long is lunch? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: From 11:35 to 12:00 is 25 minutes, then 10 more minutes to 12:10 makes 35 minutes.

questions.questionText: The bar graph shows how many shells each child found. How many shells did Tara and Omar find altogether?
questions.explanation: Tara found 15 shells and Omar found 10 shells. 15 + 10 = 25 shells altogether.

questions.questionText: Each ⚽ stands for 3 goals. How many more goals did the Tigers score than the Lions?
questions.explanation: Tigers scored 7 balls = 7 × 3 = 21 goals. Lions scored 4 balls = 4 × 3 = 12 goals. 21 − 12 = 9 more goals.

questions.questionText: What number do the base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 5 hundreds, 3 tens, and 0 ones make 500 + 30 + 0 = 530.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 4 × 8 = 32, and 32 = 20 + 12, so the blank is 20.

questions.questionText: Select all the numbers that round to 600 when rounded to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: 561 and 638 are between 550 and 649, so they round to 600. But 549 rounds to 500 and 652 rounds to 700, so those are not correct.

questions.questionText: What is 352 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: The halfway point between 300 and 400 is 350. Since 352 is just above 350, it rounds up to 400.
